name: LA Loma
description_short: La Loma is a restaurant business, not a financial services company. The website contains no financial products, lending services, or cash advance offerings.
description_long: La Loma operates as a restaurant business based on the available website content. The homepage displays standard restaurant features including a promotional contest ("Win $500! Enter by writing a dish review") and navigation elements typical of food service establishments. There is no evidence of financial services, lending products, credit offerings, or any payday alternative services on the provided website content. The website URL (lalomarestaurants.com) and homepage structure indicate this is a dining establishment, not a consumer finance company. The current categorization as "payday-alternatives" appears to be a significant miscategorization error. La Loma does not offer credit union PALs, CDFI services, employer advances, or any lending products under 36% APR as required for that category. The company's primary business is food service and restaurant operations. No financial services infrastructure, loan applications, APR disclosures, or lending documentation are present on the website.
pros: Not-for-profit — typically lower fees and better rates than banks
NCUA insured — deposits protected up to $250,000
cons: Miscategorized as a financial services company when it operates as a restaurant
No financial products or lending services offered
Website contains no information relevant to consumer finance needs
best_for: Lower fees and better loan rates than banks
diagnosis: La Loma should not be listed on CreditDoc. This is a restaurant business with no consumer finance products. The company should be removed from the database or recategorized to a non-financial services category if business directory inclusion is appropriate.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
